catalog.rationale: The revived Italian cinema emulsion translates a 1960s screen language into still-camera formats and modern coating realities.

reference.relationship: Independent interpretive emulation; researched, not measured

dossier.tagline: High silver density, rich blacks, bright skin, and tight cinema grain.
dossier.portrait: P30 is modeled around high silver density, rich blacks, bright skin, and tight cinema grain. The revived Italian cinema emulsion translates a 1960s screen language into still-camera formats and modern coating realities. Grainlab treats that identity as a linked capture, emulsion, process, and output system—not as a color cast pasted over a digital file.

facts.label: CANON RANK
facts.note: Curatorial significance rank; not a sales chart

chapters.title: Why P30 belongs in the century canon
chapters.lede: The revived Italian cinema emulsion translates a 1960s screen language into still-camera formats and modern coating realities.

details.label: Material
details.value: B&W negative; panchromatic; nominal speed / EI reference 80.

details.label: Image structure
details.value: cubic crystal interpretation, uniform emulsion mapping, fine presentation-scale grain.

notes: The canon balances cultural use, technical influence, geographic/manufacturer breadth, process diversity, and surviving documentation; rank is curatorial rather than a claimed global sales measurement.

chapters.title: Tone, color, and material signature
chapters.lede: The target is high silver density, rich blacks, bright skin, and tight cinema grain.

details.label: Curve
details.value: Toe 0.15 · development gamma 1.04 · shoulder 0.36 · scan contrast 1.00.

details.label: Color / spectral posture
details.value: Monochrome conversion uses panchromatic weighting before the film curve.

details.label: Grain
details.value: fine metallic-silver clumps; cubic geometry; uniform architecture.

details.label: Halation
details.value: 3% thresholded scene-highlight scatter before the film curve; intentionally stock/process dependent.

notes: Grain is signal-dependent and spatially correlated across the full frame; it is not independent RGB noise or a repeating overlay.

chapters.title: From DSLR or iPhone to a plausible negative
chapters.lede: Begin from a neutral SDR rendering with restrained sharpening and noise reduction. Convert through the stated spectral weights before the silver curve; do not desaturate a contrasty RGB image and call it film.

details.label: Input normalization
details.value: Prefer RAW developed neutrally when available. For HEIC/JPEG, choose the least processed rendition; avoid baked Vivid styles, portrait relighting, clarity, and denoise.

details.label: Dynamic range
details.value: Map HDR/Display-P3 into a stable SDR working image without clipping, preserving highlight and shadow separation before the film response.

details.label: White balance
details.value: Neutralize obvious camera casts first, then let the panchromatic stock balance and emulsion sensitivity create the intended response.

details.label: Hard limit
details.value: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.

notes: Apple ProRAW and camera RAW still need a deliberate neutral development; RAW is not automatically scene-linear or aesthetically neutral after a converter renders it.

chapters.title: A stage-by-stage rendering target
chapters.lede: sRGB decode → +0.00 EV scene placement → 1.000/1.000/1.000 channel sensitivity → toe 0.15, γ 1.04, shoulder 0.36 → B&W negative chemistry → 3% thresholded halation → fine silver structure

details.label: Scene
details.value: Decode to linear light, apply +0.00 EV and the stock sensitivity before tone shaping.

details.label: Emulsion
details.value: Build the toe, midtone gamma, shoulder, crossover, saturation compression, and correlated image structure as one response.

details.label: Lab / optics
details.value: Model B&W negative, chemical fog, local density contrast, and highlight scatter before the output transform.

details.label: Scan / print
details.value: Use a neutral scan baseline, set black and white points after the film curve, and sharpen subject detail at final output size.

notes: All parameters are bounded artistic approximations derived from published characteristics and process knowledge, not digitized manufacturer sensitometric or spectral measurements.

bestFor: Subjects that benefit from high silver density, rich blacks, bright skin, and tight cinema grain.
Form, gesture, texture, and light where color separation can be translated into deliberate gray relationships.
watchFor: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.
Stacking a strong camera picture style, computational HDR, or sharpening underneath the emulation.
Judging grain at 200% instead of at 100% and intended output size.
fieldNotes: Expose for the material idea: high silver density, rich blacks, bright skin, and tight cinema grain.
Try the profile on neutral daylight, mixed light, skin, foliage, and a flat wall before deciding what its name means.
dossier.disclaimer: P30 is an independent Grainlab artistic emulation informed by cited public literature and historical material. It is not endorsed by Film Ferrania, is not a measured ICC/LUT profile, and cannot reproduce a specific batch, lens, exposure, chemistry, printer, scanner, paper, aging state, or wavelengths absent from the digital source.
